what_it_does: A decisions log that records settled and in-progress design choices for the Concurrent Cognition subsystem, organized into locked items, answered batches, developer-level calls, and build-readiness status.
observations: Locked decisions declare the system must be registry-driven, not hardcoded, with rule-based routing/resolution as the core mechanism.

Open decisions are grouped into answered batches covering part grain, role casts, build scope, rule grammar, slot budgets, model residency, cloud coupling, activation contexts, role authority, destinations, jury/ensemble, live view, and rule gating.
The first locked role cast for the listening mode contains six roles: focus, recall, ground, connect, check, voice.
Developer-level calls record unresolved micro-decisions such as address schemes, swarm namespace lifecycle, tools arrays, and per-mode part-grain mappings.
Status is marked BUILD-READY after two review rounds, with a G0 proving spike gating further fan-out.
Decisions are attributed to Tim with dates and framed by system laws like deterministic, declarative-by-inspection, renderable, no hidden model judgment, and no silent fallback.
standouts: The spine is deterministic registry-declared rule routing, not model-quality aggregation or fusion.
Roles are mode-scoped registry data; the resident swarm, main brain choice, and mode-driven routing are treated as orthogonal axes.
Routing rules are rich predicates plus composition, evaluated by a restricted AST evaluator with resolved-values-only and no eval().
The first build must include the live cognition thought-graph on the canvas, not defer rendering.
conventions: Decision-log format with locked/open sections and batch Q&A.
Uppercase emphasis for chosen options and key constraints.
Attribution of each decision to a named decider and date.
References to external system laws, folds, and prior decisions using shorthand codes (R2-FOLD, H2, R4, rule-8, etc.).
Status progression from READY TO CONVERGE to BUILD-READY.
concerns: Many design decisions and unresolved sub-dev-calls are compressed into one markdown file, which may become hard to navigate as implementation proceeds.
Several shorthand references (R2-FOLD, H2, B3-R1, [[feedback-autonomous-spawn-lead-only]]) are not defined inside the file.
The accepted build scope is a full substrate rebuild before any application payoff, creating a large upfront commitment.
Future-dated entries (2026-06-07) appear in a log whose context implies current decision-making.
notes: This is the authoritative decision trail for the concurrent-cognition rebuild; read it to know what is locked, what was deferred, and what the first proving spike must demonstrate.
questions: What are the exact registry schemas for roles, rules, edges, ports, and output_schema?
What are the concrete acceptance criteria and measurement method for the G0 proving spike?
What are the precise responsibilities of the six listening-mode roles?
How does the restricted rule evaluator represent and type-check predicates over resolved values?
What are the two landscapes and loop-prep triad this file repeatedly references?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to record Tim's answers as a decisions log; the file does that and also acts as a build-readiness checkpoint tracking status and developer calls, slightly exceeding a pure decision log.
apparent_themes: Deterministic routing instead of model judgment.
Governance through declared rules and existing change discipline.
intention_signals: It is trying to converge a large, ambiguous concurrent-cognition design into a concrete, buildable substrate by locking architectural principles, scoping the first build, and delegating detailed design to sub-agent-driven implementation while preserving traceability of every choice.
novelty: The explicit rejection of model-aggregation as the spine in favor of deterministic registry-declared rule routing, and the orthogonal treatment of resident swarm, main brain, and mode-driven auto-routing.
contribution: A timestamped, attributed decision trail that locks the architectural spine (registry-driven rule-based routing), defines the first role cast and build scope, and records the constraints and laws the implementation must obey; it gives a fused system a clear starting point and guardrails without claiming primacy.
summary_for_embedding: A decisions log for the Concurrent Cognition subsystem that records locked and answered design choices about registry-driven rule-based routing, mode-scoped roles, resident/cloud brain separation, and build-readiness status.
intention_for_embedding: To capture Tim's directed design decisions and developer calls so the sub-agent-driven build in the company-cognition worktree has a hardened reference for the first proving spike.
